questions.text: Nowadays every newborn gets regular shots of vaccine for polio. It contains _ for polio to make a child immune against this disease.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Antibiotics are a class of medications used to treat bacterial infections. They work by either killing bacteria or inhibiting their growth and reproduction. Antibiotics are specifically designed to target bacteria and have no effect on viruses.

Antibodies, also known as immunoglobulins, are proteins produced by the immune system in response to the presence of foreign substances called antigens. Antibodies play a crucial role in the immune response and provide defense against harmful pathogens, such as bacteria, viruses, and other microorganisms.

The vaccine for polio contains antigens, specifically the inactivated or weakened forms of the poliovirus. These antigens stimulate the immune system to produce an immune response, including the production of antibodies, without causing the actual disease.

Antisera is a blood-derived product that contains antibodies specifically targeted against a particular antigen or group of antigens. It is obtained from the blood of animals or humans that have been immunized with the antigen of interest.

questions.explanation: A vaccine elicits an immune response by the host so that the host is better equipped to fight against the invading organism, in any potential future instance, via the production of memory cells. It may do so by containing the antigens of the invading organism (in this case, poliovirus) so that the host’s immune system produces memory cells against the specific organism, thereby conferring active immunity against the disease. Antibiotics (option A) are not present in vaccines; they are drugs used to treat bacterial illnesses. Antibodies (option B) are not present in vaccines; they are proteins, produced by plasma cells, to ward off infection. They are produced by the immune system to fight an active infection, but they are not used in vaccines since they do not confer active immunity as the host does not produce memory cells against the invading organism. Antisera (option D) is a blood serum preparation that contains antibodies against a certain antigen or disease; they are used as a treatment for certain conditions and do not confer active immunity to the individual.

questions.text: Chicken pox is caused by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hepatitis A virus (HAV) primarily causes liver inflammation and is transmitted through contaminated food and water, not through direct contact or respiratory means associated with chickenpox.

The varicella zoster virus (VZV) is the causative agent of chickenpox, known for its characteristic itchy rash and blisters. It spreads easily through respiratory droplets and direct contact with the lesions.
Although the influenza virus leads to respiratory symptoms like fever and cough, it does not cause the distinct skin rash typical of chickenpox.
The human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) affects the immune system and is transmitted through bodily fluids. It does not cause chickenpox or its symptoms.

questions.explanation: Chickenpox (varicella) is an infectious disease caused by the vancella zoster virus (VZV), a member of the herpesvirus family. The virus leads to an itchy rash and fluid-filled blisters, typically in children. The same virus can reactivate later in life as shingles.

questions.text: The viruses are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viruses are not considered cellular organisms because they lack structures essential to cells, such as a cell membrane and organelles. They cannot independently carry out life processes like metabolism or reproduction.

Prokaryotes, such as bacteria, are unicellular organisms with a simpler structure than eukaryotes but still have cell membranes and cytoplasm. Viruses lack these features and are not classified as prokaryotes.

Viruses are non-cellular entities made up of genetic material encased in a protein coat. They do not possess cellular structures or the ability to perform cellular functions independently, making them distinct from all cellular life forms.
Eukaryotes have complex cells with a nucleus and organelles. Viruses lack these cellular components and cannot perform the functions of eukaryotic cells, such as energy production and reproduction.

Viruses are extremely small, typically ranging from 20 to 300 nanometers, which is far too small to be seen without a microscope. Observing viruses requires advanced techniques like electron microscopy.

questions.explanation: Viruses are classified as non-cellular because they lack the cellular structures that define living cells, such as a cell membrane, cytoplasm, and organelles. Instead, they consist of genetic material (either DNA or RNA) surrounded by a protein coat called a capsid. Unlike cellular organisms, viruses cannot perform metabolic processes or reproduce on their own; they must infect a host cell to replicate. This distinguishes them from cellular life forms, including prokaryotes and eukaryotes.Option A (Cellular) is incorrect because viruses do not have the structures that define cellular life. Option B (Prokaryotes) is incorrect because viruses do not fit the definition of prokaryotic cells, which have a cell membrane and cytoplasm. Option D (Eukaryotes) is incorrect because viruses do not have the complex structures found in eukaryotic cells. Option E (Visible with naked eye) is incorrect because viruses are too small to be seen without specialized equipment.

questions.text: Which of the following is the simplest form of pathogens causing diseases?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viruses, while simple compared to many other life forms, still consist of genetic material (DNA or RNA) encapsulated in a protein coat. They require a host cell to reproduce and cannot independently cause disease, making them more complex than prions.

Prions are infectious agents that consist solely of misfolded proteins without any nucleic acid. They are considered the simplest form of pathogens because they lack cellular structure and replication machinery, which distinguishes them from other pathogens.

Fungi are complex organisms with defined cellular structures and reproductive mechanisms. They require specific environmental conditions to grow and are not the simplest pathogens, as they are multicellular or unicellular with extensive biological functions.

Amoebas are single-celled organisms that can cause diseases, but they are more complex than prions, as they possess cellular structures, organelles, and various pathogenic mechanisms. Thus, they do not represent the simplest form of pathogens.

questions.explanation: Prions are the simplest disease-causing pathogens because they are merely misfolded proteins with with no nucleic acid (no DNA or RNA), making them the most minimal infectious agents compared to viruses, fungi, or protozoa like amoeba.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a viral disease?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: AIDS is a viral illness that develops when a person is infected with the human immunodeficiency virus, or HIV. This virus weakens the immune system over time, making the body more vulnerable to infections and certain cancers.
Malaria is not a viral illness. It’s caused by protozoa of the genus Plasmodium, which are transmitted to humans through the bite of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
Influenza is a viral infection, and it’s caused by the influenza virus, which mainly affects the respiratory tract.
Chickenpox is a viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus. It typically leads to an itchy, blister-like rash along with fever and fatigue.

questions.explanation: Malaria is not a viral disease and is caused by the protozoan Plasmodium. All other options are viral infections that include AIDS caused by the HIV virus, influenza caused by the influenza virus, chickenpox caused by the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), and rabies caused by the rabies lyssavirus.

questions.text: HIV that causes AIDS first starts destroying:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because the AIDS retrovirus, called human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), mounts a direct attack on CD4+ T-helper cells because it recognises the CD4 coreceptors associated with these cells. HIV’s attack on CD4 + T cells cripples the immune system in at least three ways. First, HIV-infected cells die only after releasing replicated viruses that infect other CD4+ T cells, ultimately destroying the entire population of CD4+ T cells. Second, HIV causes infected CD4 + T cells, to secrete a soluble suppressing factor that blocks other T cells from responding to the HIV antigen. Finally, HIV may block transcription of MHC genes, hindering the recognition and destruction of infected CD4 + T cells, and thus protecting those cells from any remaining vestiges of the immune system. The combined effect of these responses to HIV infection is to wipe out the human immune defence.
This option is incorrect because Platelets are called thrombocytes and have no relation to HIV.
This option is incorrect because lymphocytes are cells of the lymphatic system.
This option is incorrect because leukocytes are white blood cells and have no relation to HIV.

options.explanation: This option is correct because the common cold is not cured by antibiotics because it is primarily caused by viruses, not bacteria. Antibiotics are effective against bacterial infections but do not work against viral infections.

options.explanation: This option is correct because the common cold is not caused by a gram-positive bacterium; it is caused by a virus.

options.text: Caused by a Gram negative bacterium
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the common cold is not a bacterial disease.
This option is incorrect because the common cold is an infectious disease and not cured by antibiotics, as it is primarily caused by viruses, not bacteria.

questions.explanation: The common cold is caused by some 100 types of rhinoviruses. It is one of the most common infectious diseases in humans. Antibiotics are substances that destroy or inhibit the growth of microorganisms, particularly disease-producing bacteria and fungi. Antibiotics are obtained from microorganisms (especially molds) or synthesised. Many antibiotics interfere with the pathogen's protein synthesis. Some (e.g., penicillin) prevent cross-linking of the glycan chains of peptidoglycan molecules in bacterial cell walls. Since the viruses do not possess cell walls or their own protein-synthesizing apparatus, antibiotics do not attack them.

questions.text: Infectious proteins are present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because geminiviruses are a group of plant-infecting viruses characterized by their unique twin (geminate) particle structure. They do not consist of proteins alone, as viruses typically have a protein coat and genetic material.

This option is correct because prions are infectious agents made up of misfolded proteins. They can lead to neurodegenerative diseases by promoting the misfolding of normal proteins in the brain, making them unique among infectious agents.

This option is incorrect because viroids are the smallest known infectious agents and consist solely of short strands of circular, single-stranded RNA. They lack a protein coat and do not involve proteins in their structure.

This option is incorrect because satellite viruses are subviral agents that depend on a helper virus for replication. They do not consist solely of proteins, as they require a viral coat and cannot independently function.

questions.explanation: This option is correct because Prions are the infectious agents composed of abnormal misfolded proteins that have the ability to induce similar misfolding of normal proteins in the brain.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because the common cold is caused by the common cold virus (rhinovirus), and AIDS is caused by HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus).
This option is incorrect because the common cold is caused by viruses, whereas dysentery can be caused by either bacteria or parasites, but not viruses.
This option is incorrect because typhoid is caused by Salmonella typhi. Tuberculosis is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Typhoid and tuberculosis are bacterial diseases, not viral.
This option is incorrect because AIDS is a viral disease caused by the HIV virus. On the other hand, ringworm is a fungal disease, not viral.

questions.explanation: The common cold is a viral disease. It is caused by rhinoviruses. It causes fever and pain all over the body and affects the nose, throat, and air passages. AIDS (acquired immunodeficiency syndrome) is a disorder of the cell-mediated immune system of the body. It is caused by HIV (human immunodeficiency virus). HIV is a retrovirus that attacks helper T cells.

questions.text: Lysozyme that is present in perspiration, saliva and tears, destroys :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lysozyme is an antibacterial enzyme with natural antibiotic properties. Normally excreted in the tears, nasal mucus, milk, and saliva in most animals, lysozyme is part of the body’s first natural defense against bacteria and viruses. Lysozyme is an enzyme that degrades the polysaccharide protective coating on the surface of many bacteria and viruses (glycoprotein covering) to allow other enzymes and antibodies to find their appropriate attachment site. Most of the bacteria affected by lysozyme are not pathogenic. Lysozyme serves as a nonspecific innate opsonize by binding to the bacterial surface, reducing the negative charge, and facilitating phagocytosis of the bacterium before opsonins from the acquired immune system arrive at the scene. In other words, lysozyme makes it easier for phagocytic white blood cells to engulf bacteria.

Lysozymes are specific to the structure of bacterial cell walls, primarily targeting peptidoglycan, a component unique to bacteria. Viruses and fungi have different structures and are not susceptible to this mode of action.
Viruses are not considered as complete cells , as they lack cell wall. Since they lack cell wall , lysozymes have no target structure to act upon.
Fungi have complex cell wall. While lysozymes may affect some fungi, fungal cell walls are more complex than bacterial cell wall and may not be suspectable for lysozyme action.

questions.text: The causative agent of mad cow disease is a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mad cow disease is not caused by a virus. Viruses contain genetic material (DNA or RNA) and replicate within host cells. In contrast, the causative agent of mad cow disease lacks any genetic material and spreads through the misfolding of proteins, which is characteristic of prions.

Mad cow disease is not the result of a bacterial infection. Bacteria are living organisms that contain DNA and typical cellular structures, which are absent in prions. The agent responsible for mad cow disease is a prion, a misfolded protein that induces abnormal folding in normal proteins, unlike bacteria that can be treated with antibiotics.

The correct answer is prion, as mad cow disease, or bovine spongiform encephalopathy (BSE), is caused by these infectious proteins. Prions are unique because they do not contain nucleic acids; instead, they propagate by inducing normal proteins in the brain to misfold, leading to severe neurological damage.

Worms, such as helminths, are parasitic organisms that do not cause mad cow disease. They are multicellular organisms that can infect hosts, but they are fundamentally different from prions, which are solely misfolded proteins, lacking any cellular structure.

questions.explanation: Prions are infectious proteins that cause abnormal folding of normal brain proteins, leading to brain damage.

questions.text: A rider on a horse back falls when the horse starts running all of a sudden because:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The rider is pushed backward by the air resistance
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Air resistance would not be significant enough to cause the rider to fall backward in this scenario. The primary factor is inertia, not air resistance.

options.text: The rider becomes suddenly afraid due to the horse's movement
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While fear might be a factor psychologically, the physical reason the rider falls is due to inertia, not emotion.

options.text: Inertia of rest keeps the rider's upper body stationary while the lower body moves with the horse
options.explanation: This is correct. When the horse suddenly starts to run, the upper part of the rider's body tends to remain at rest due to inertia, causing the rider to lose balance and fall.
This is incorrect. The correct explanation is related to the inertia of rest affecting the rider's body.

questions.explanation: When the horse starts running suddenly, the rider's body is subject to the law of inertia, which states that an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by an external force. The rider's upper body remains at rest due to inertia while the lower body moves forward with the horse. This difference in motion causes the rider to lose balance and fall. This phenomenon is a direct application of Newton's first law of motion.The incorrect options either misattribute the cause to air resistance or emotion, which are not the primary factors in this physical scenario.

questions.text: A machine gun fires a bullet of mass 40 g with a velocity 1200 m/s. The man holding it can exert a maximum force of 144 N on the gun. How many bullets can he fire per second at the most?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the man can only fire one bullet per second, it would mean the total force exerted per second is less than the maximum force he can handle. This option does not utilize the maximum force capacity.
Firing four bullets per second would require a force that exceeds the man's maximum capacity of 144 N. Thus, this option is incorrect.
Firing two bullets per second results in a force less than 144 N, but it does not maximize the potential firing rate based on the maximum force available.
Firing three bullets per second results in a force exactly equal to the man's maximum capacity of 144 N, making this option correct.

questions.explanation: To solve this problem, we use the principle of momentum. The momentum imparted by each bullet is given by mass × velocity = 0.04 kg × 1200 m/s = 48 kg·m/s. The change in momentum per second is equal to the force applied: F = Δp/Δt. If the man fires 'n' bullets per second, the total momentum change per second is n × 48 kg·m/s. Setting this equal to the maximum force, 144 N = n × 48, we find n = 3. Therefore, the correct answer is three bullets per second. The other options either underestimate or overestimate the number of bullets based on the force calculation.

questions.text: When a particle moves in a vertical circle:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: It has constant radial and tangential acceleration
options.explanation: In a vertical circle, both radial and tangential accelerations change due to varying gravitational and centripetal forces at different points. Thus, accelerations are not constant.

options.text: It has variable tangential and radial accelerations
options.explanation: In a vertical circle, such as when an object is swinging on a string or a roller coaster is moving along a loop, the motion is not characterized by constant acceleration. The direction of motion and the forces acting on the object change throughout the motion. As a result, both tangential (in the direction of motion) and radial (inward or outward) accelerations vary as the object moves through the different points of the circle. At the top of the circle, for instance, the tangential acceleration may be directed downward, while at the bottom, it may be directed upward, resulting in varying acceleration values. Similarly, the radial acceleration changes as the object moves around the circle. This makes option (b) the correct choice.

options.text: It only has constant radial acceleration
options.explanation: Radial acceleration varies as the speed of the particle changes at different points in the vertical circle, especially due to gravitational effects at the top and bottom of the loop.

options.text: It only has constant tangential acceleration
options.explanation: Tangential acceleration changes as the particle speeds up or slows down due to gravity, making it variable rather than constant.

questions.explanation: In a vertical circular motion, such as a particle on a string or a roller coaster loop, both radial and tangential accelerations vary. The gravitational force acting on the particle changes direction relative to the motion, affecting both accelerations. At the top of the circle, gravity causes the tangential component to point downward, while at the bottom, it points upward. Similarly, the radial acceleration is affected by the changing speed due to gravitational influence. This makes Option B correct as it accurately describes the nature of the motion. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because they incorrectly assume that one or both accelerations remain constant.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 0.7 kg/s. The thrust force exerted by the rocket engine is given by the formula F = rate of fuel combustion × velocity of exhaust gases. Here, F = 210 N and velocity = 300 m/s. Solving for the rate of fuel combustion gives 210 N ÷ 300 m/s = 0.7 kg/s. Other options result from misapplying or miscalculating this formula.

questions.text: A man is standing on a weighing machine placed in a lift. When stationary, his mass is recorded as 40 kg. If the lift is moved upwards with an acceleration of 2ms-2, then the weight recorded in the machine will be: (g = 10 ms-2)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. This indicates a misunderstanding of the upward acceleration's effect on weight, as it reduces rather than increases the apparent weight.
Incorrect. This represents the actual weight when stationary, not accounting for the lift's upward acceleration.
Incorrect. This is a miscalculation of the apparent weight, given the upward acceleration.
This is the following solution: N = m (g + a) N = 40 (10 + 2) N = 480 newton N = 48 kg–wt.

questions.explanation: To determine the apparent weight of the man when the lift is accelerating upwards, we use the formula for normal force (apparent weight) in an accelerating frame: N = m(g + a), where m is the mass, g is the acceleration due to gravity, and a is the acceleration of the lift. Given that m = 40 kg, g = 10 ms-2, and a = 2 ms-2, substituting these values gives: N = 40 (10 + 2) = 480 newton Since weight is typically expressed in kg-wt, we convert this to 48 kg-wt. Therefore, the correct answer is 48 kg. All other options either do not account for the lift's acceleration or miscalculate its effect, resulting in incorrect apparent weight values.

questions.text: A body is moving with a speed of 1ms–1 and a force F is needed to stop it in a distance x. If the speed of the body is 3 ms–1, the force needed to stop it in the same distance x will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the force required to stop the body is not linear with speed. The kinetic energy, which is proportional to the square of the speed, determines the stopping force.
This option is incorrect because the stopping force is related to the square of the speed, not directly proportional to the speed. Therefore, this underestimate the required force.
This option is incorrect because it does not accurately reflect the relationship between the speed and the stopping force, which is quadratic, not linear.
This option is correct. The stopping force needed is proportional to the square of the speed. Since the speed is tripled from 1 m/s to 3 m/s, the force becomes 9 times greater, as (3^2) = 9.

questions.explanation: To solve this problem, consider the relationship between kinetic energy and stopping force. The kinetic energy of a moving body is given by KE = (1/2)mv^2. To stop the body, this kinetic energy must be dissipated by the stopping force over the distance x. Therefore, the stopping force is proportional to the square of the speed, v^2. In the first scenario, the body moves at 1 m/s, and a force F is needed to stop it. In the second scenario, the speed is 3 m/s, which means the force required is proportional to (3^2), or 9 times the original force. Hence, the correct answer is 9F.The other options (1.5F, 3F, and 6F) do not correctly account for the quadratic relationship between speed and the stopping force required.

questions.text: If the engine power is 3.3kW and it is 60% efficient, how much water will it pump in 5s from a height of 10m?

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect according to calculations.

To calculate the amount of water pumped in 5 seconds from a height of 10 meters, we can use the principle of work and energy. First, let's determine the potential energy of the water at a height of 10 meters.Potential energy (P.E) = mass (m) * gravitational acceleration (g) * height (h)where:g ≈ 9.81 m/s² (gravitational acceleration)Given that the potential energy is transferred into the water by the engine, and the engine is 60% efficient, we can calculate the work done by the engine( Wengine) as follows:Wengine = P.E/efficiency , work done by the engine is also equal to the power (P) of the engine multiplied by time (t):Wengine = P * tWe are given that the engine power (P) is 3.3 kW, and the time (t) is 5 seconds.Now we can set up the equation:3.3 kW * 5 s = PE / 0.60Solving for PE:P.E = 3.3 kW * 5 s * 0.60 ≈ 9.9 kJNow, we can find the mass of the water (m) using the potential energy (P.E):PE = m * g * h9.9 kJ = m * 9.81 m/s² * 10 mSolving for m:m = 9.9 kJ / (9.81 m/s² * 10 m) ≈ 0.101 kg ≈ 100 g

questions.text: You accidentally bump into a bookcase. It tilts briefly but then returns to upright, and you breath a sigh of relief. When you first bumped the bookcase, its center of gravity moved:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Upward and its gravitational potential energy increased.
options.explanation: Upward and its gravitational potential energy increased. The bookcase is in a stable equilibrium, as evidenced by its tendency to return to that equilibrium after being bumped. To obtain this stable equilibrium, the bookcase's potential energy must increase as it tilts so that it accelerates in the direction that reduces its potential energy as quickly as possible--namely back toward equilibrium. That arrangement requires that its center of gravity to rise, since gravitational potential energy is the only form of potential energy in this situation.

options.text: Downward and its gravitational potential energy decreased.
options.explanation: Incorrect. If the center of gravity moved downward, the bookcase would be in an unstable equilibrium, and it would not return to its original position. The gravitational potential energy would decrease, contrary to what is observed.

options.text: Downward and its gravitational potential energy increased.
options.explanation: Incorrect. A downward movement of the center of gravity would indicate a decrease in potential energy, not an increase, thus not supporting the stable equilibrium observed.

options.text: Upward and its gravitational potential energy decreased.
options.explanation: Incorrect. An upward movement of the center of gravity would lead to an increase in gravitational potential energy, not a decrease. This is consistent with the stable equilibrium described.
Incorrect. Option A correctly describes the movement and energy change of the bookcase's center of gravity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the center of gravity moved upward and its gravitational potential energy increased. This increase in potential energy indicates that the bookcase is in a stable equilibrium, as it returns to its upright position after being displaced. This is consistent with the characteristics of stable equilibrium, where a displacement leads to an increase in potential energy, prompting the system to revert to its equilibrium state. The other options are incorrect as they do not align with the principles of stable equilibrium and the observed behavior of the bookcase.

questions.explanation: Bacterial spores, especially endospores, are highly resistant structures formed by certain bacteria such as those in the genera Bacillus and Clostridium. The statement that spores 'can never germinate into vegetative cells' is incorrect, making it the correct choice in this context. This option is incorrect because the nucleoid is the region of the bacterial cell containing DNA, and it does not become dormant or resistant to desiccation.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Cysts. Cysts are specialized structures that bacteria form as a survival mechanism under adverse environmental conditions. They are characterized by being dormant, having thick walls, and being resistant to desiccation. These features enable the bacteria to endure until conditions become favorable again. In contrast, granules, ribosomes, and nucleoids do not serve as protective structures with these characteristics. Granules are for storage, ribosomes are for protein synthesis, and nucleoids hold genetic material.

questions.text: The bacteria which get their food from dead organic matter are called as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because heterotrophs encompass a wide range of organisms that consume organic compounds, including those from living organisms, not exclusively from dead organic matter.
This option is correct as saprotrophic bacteria, or saprophytes, specifically thrive on decomposing dead organic matter, playing a crucial role in nutrient cycling in ecosystems.
This option is incorrect because parasitic bacteria derive their nutrients from a living host organism, often harming it in the process, rather than consuming dead organic matter.
This option is incorrect because autotrophic bacteria synthesize their own food from inorganic substances and do not depend on dead organic matter for their nutritional needs.

questions.explanation: This option is correct because Saprotrophic bacteria. known as saprophytic bacteria, are a subset of heterotrophic. bacteria. They obtain their nutrition by decomposing and feeding on dead organic matter, such as decaying plant material or animal remains.

questions.text: Bacteria that can grow in the absence of oxygen are called as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because anaerobes are bacteria that can grow and thrive in environments devoid of oxygen. They utilize fermentation or anaerobic respiration to generate energy, making them well-suited for anaerobic conditions.

This option is incorrect because molds are not bacteria; they are eukaryotic fungi. Molds generally require oxygen for their metabolic processes and do not fall under the category of microorganisms that can thrive without oxygen.

This option is incorrect because aerobes are bacteria that require oxygen for their growth and energy production. They rely on aerobic respiration, which makes them incapable of surviving in the absence of oxygen.
This option is incorrect because yeasts are single-celled fungi, not bacteria. While some yeasts can perform fermentation to grow without oxygen, they are not classified as anaerobic bacteria.

questions.explanation: This option is correct because Anaerobic bacteria are microorganisms that can grow and thrive in the absence of oxygen. They can alternative metabolic pathways to generate energy without relying on oxygen.

questions.text: If we say that antibiotics kill the bacteria when it is rapidly dividing and growing phase, then an antibiotic penicillin will kill the bacteria during which phase?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. In the stationary phase, the growth rate slows due to depleted resources, leading to a balance between dying and new cells. Antibiotics are less effective here since they primarily target actively dividing cells.

This option is incorrect. During the lag phase, bacteria are adjusting to their new environment and are not dividing rapidly. Therefore, antibiotics that target cell division are generally ineffective in this phase.

This option is correct. The log phase, or exponential phase, is when bacteria divide at their maximum rate. Antibiotics like penicillin are most effective during this phase because they inhibit cell wall synthesis, which is crucial for rapid cell division.

This option is incorrect. In the death phase, the number of dying cells exceeds new cell production. Although antibiotics may still affect the bacteria, their efficacy is significantly reduced as the overall population declines.

questions.explanation: In this phase, bacteria are actively dividing and growing at their maximum rate. Antibiotics are most effective during this phase because they target rapidly dividing cells.

questions.text: The process in which we use physical agents to control microorganism is termed as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because immunization refers to the process of stimulating the immune system to protect against specific pathogens, generally through vaccines. It does not involve the direct control or elimination of microorganisms using physical methods.

This option is correct because sterilization is the method that employs physical agents such as heat, radiation, or filtration to completely eliminate all forms of microorganisms, including bacteria and viruses, from an environment or surface.

This option is incorrect as vaccination is a preventive strategy that introduces antigens to stimulate an immune response, rather than a technique for controlling or eliminating microorganisms through physical means.

This option is incorrect because chemical methods involve the use of substances like disinfectants to control microorganisms. The question specifically asks for techniques involving physical agents, which excludes chemical means.

questions.explanation: This option is correct because Sterilization is the process of using physical or chemical methods to completely eliminate or kill all forms of microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and spores, from a surface or substance. It is a method for controlling microorganisms by making an environment completely free of them.

questions.text: In Cyanobacteria, all these structures are present except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because Glycogen is present in cyanobacteria. It is a storage polysaccharide found in many bacteria, including cyanobacteria. It serves as an energy reserve and is typically present in cyanobacteria.

This option is incorrect because akinetes are present in cyanobacteria. These are specialized, thick-walled, dormant cells produced by some cyanobacteria. They help the cyanobacteria survive adverse environmental conditions. Akinetes are indeed a structure found in cyanobacteria.

This option is incorrect because Phycobilins are pigment molecules found in cyanobacteria and other photosynthetic organisms. They play a crucial role in capturing light energy during photosynthesis. Phycobilins are typically present in cyanobacteria.

options.text: Jelly like mass with numerous filaments
options.explanation: The correct answer is that "Jelly-like mass with numerous filaments" is not present in cyanobacteria.

questions.explanation: Cyanobacteria are a group of photosynthetic bacteria that possess various structures and forms. Some common structures found in cyanobacteria include:Filaments: Cyanobacteria often form filamentous structures, where individual cells are connected in a chain-like arrangement. These filaments can be unicellular or multicellular.Unicellular Forms: Some cyanobacteria exist as individual cells or colonies of cells that do not form filaments.Heterocysts: Certain cyanobacteria possess specialized cells called heterocysts. Heterocysts are involved in nitrogen fixation, converting atmospheric nitrogen into a form that can be utilized by the organism.Sheaths: Cyanobacteria may secrete protective sheaths around their filaments. These sheaths provide structural support and protection.However, a "jelly-like mass" is not a typical characteristic of cyanobacteria. Cyanobacteria are primarily characterized by their filamentous or unicellular forms, along with the presence of specialized structures like heterocysts and sheaths.

questions.text: The minimum energy below which no reaction occurs on reactant molecules is the:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Average kinetic energy of the molecule
options.explanation: More or less than the average kinetic energy, a reaction can still take place.
Potential energy is the energy due to position or chemical bonds, not specifically the threshold for reaction.
Free energy (Gibbs free energy) determines spontaneity of the reaction, but not the minimum energy needed for a collision.
The minimum energy below which no reaction occurs on reactant molecules is known as the activation energy.

questions.explanation: For a chemical reaction to occur, reactant molecules must collide with sufficient energy to overcome the energy barrier.This energy barrier is called the activation energy (Ea).Molecules with energy below Ea will not react, no matter how many collisions occur.

questions.text: Higher the activation energy of the reaction:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Slow is the rate of reaction
options.explanation: Specifically, the higher the activation energy, the slower the chemical reaction will be. This is because molecules can only complete the reaction once they have reached the top of the activation energy barrier.

options.text: Fast is the rate of reaction
options.explanation: The higher the activation energy, the slower the chemical reaction will be. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Moderate is the rate of reaction
options.explanation: Incorrect. The rate depends directly on Ea; it’s not automatically “moderate.”

options.text: Activation energy is not related to the rate of reaction
options.explanation: Incorrect. Activation energy is directly related to the reaction rate via the Arrhenius equation

questions.explanation: Higher activation energy means fewer molecules have sufficient energy to react → slower reaction

. The transition state is at the highest potential energy point, making it highly unstable.

questions.explanation: The transition state (or activated complex) occurs at the maximum of the potential energy diagram along the reaction coordinate.It is a very short-lived, high-energy state where bonds are partially broken and partially formed.

Completely correct. All properties of the transition state are included

questions.explanation: The transition state (activated complex) is a hypothetical, high-energy structure formed during a chemical reaction at the point of maximum potential energy.Properties of the transition state:Loose structure – bonds are partially broken and partially formed. Ability to rotate – Yes, transition state has rotational freedom of parts of the molecule. Ability to vibrate – Yes, atoms can vibrate along the reaction coordinate and perpendicular to it.

questions.text: The unit of rate constant is same as that of rate of reaction in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In a first-order reaction, the rate law is expressed as Rate = k[A]. The unit of the rate constant (k) is time-1, while the unit of the rate of reaction is concentration unit per time, such as mol dm-3 s-1. Therefore, the units are not the same.

In a zero-order reaction, the rate law is Rate = k. The rate constant (k) has the same unit as the rate of reaction, which is usually concentration unit per time, such as mol dm-3 s-1. Thus, the units match.

For a second-order reaction, the rate law might be Rate = k[A]2. The unit of the rate constant (k) is typically (concentration unit)-1 * time-1, whereas the rate of reaction is concentration unit per time. Therefore, the units do not match.

In a third-order reaction, the rate law can be expressed as Rate = k[A]3. The unit of the rate constant (k) is (concentration unit)-2 * time-1. The rate of reaction has a unit of concentration unit per time. Clearly, the units are not the same.

questions.explanation: Rate Law: Rate = k The unit of the rate constant (k) is typically expressed as concentration unit per time. The unit of the rate of reaction (Rate) is also in concentration unit per time. Therefore, in a zero-order reaction, the unit of the rate constant is the same as the unit of the rate of reaction.

questions.text: It gives shape to the bacterial cell:

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While the cell membrane plays a crucial role in controlling substance movement and maintaining cellular integrity, it does not provide the rigidity or shape that characterizes bacterial cells.

This option is incorrect. The capsule serves as a protective layer that aids in evading host immune responses and adhering to surfaces, but it does not contribute to the overall shape of the bacterial cell.

This option is correct. The cell wall is a robust, rigid structure that not only defines the shape of bacterial cells but also provides protection against environmental stresses, making it essential for the cell's structural integrity.

This option is incorrect. Slime is a loosely associated substance that some bacteria produce for adhesion and protection. However, it does not have a structural role in defining the shape of the bacterial cell.

questions.explanation: The cell wall is the structure responsible for giving shape to bacterial cells is a rigid layer that surrounds the cell membrane and provides structural support and protection. The composition of the cell wall varies among different types of bacteria.

questions.text: Which one of the following is a specific structure of bacterial cell?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while the bacterial cell wall is unique in its composition of peptidoglycan, cell walls are also found in plant cells, making it not exclusive to bacteria.
Although the cell membrane is essential for bacterial cells, it is not a unique feature as it is present in all cell types, including animal, plant, and other microorganisms.
Ribosomes are universal structures found in all living cells for protein synthesis, including bacteria, plants, and animals. Therefore, this option is not specific to bacteria.
This option is correct. The capsule is a distinctive feature of some bacterial cells, providing an additional protective layer and aiding in adherence, which is not found in plant or animal cells.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the capsule, a unique structure found in some bacterial cells. The capsule is a gelatinous layer composed of polysaccharides or polypeptides, surrounding the bacterial cell wall. It offers protection from environmental stresses and immune responses, and assists in attachment to surfaces. Unlike the capsule, the cell wall and cell membrane are present in multiple organisms, and ribosomes are universal to all living cells, making them incorrect choices for this question.

questions.text: It is usually found in bacteria:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while a cell wall is a common structure in bacteria, it is not unique to them and is also found in plants, fungi, and some protists. It is not the feature that is usually highlighted specifically for bacteria in this context.

This option is incorrect because slime is produced by some bacteria, but it is not a defining or universally present feature in bacteria. It serves specific functions like protection and movement, but not all bacteria produce slime.

This option is incorrect because chromatin bodies are associated with eukaryotic cells, where they are involved in DNA packaging within the nucleus. Bacteria do not have a defined nucleus, and thus do not contain chromatin bodies.

This option is correct because mesosomes are structures usually found in bacteria. They are invaginations of the plasma membrane and play roles in cellular processes such as respiration and cell division. However, the existence and function of mesosomes are debated in scientific literature.

questions.explanation: The correct option is D) Mesosome. Mesosomes are typically found in bacteria and are characterized by invaginations of the plasma membrane. They are involved in important cellular processes like respiration and cell division. Although the significance of mesosomes is debated, they are more associated with bacteria than the other options listed. In contrast, cell walls are not unique to bacteria, slime is not ubiquitously present, and chromatin bodies are related to eukaryotic cells, not bacteria.

Bacteria can range in size from 0.5 to 5 micrometers and can take on different shapes, such as spheres (cocci), rods (bacilli), and spirals (spirilla). questions.explanation: According to Newton's First Law of Motion, an object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by an external force. When the bus suddenly accelerates forward, the person standing on it experiences inertia, meaning their body tends to resist the change in motion. The lower body is pushed forward by the bus, while the upper body, due to inertia, stays at rest momentarily. This results in the person appearing to move backward relative to the bus. Options A, B, and C do not correctly describe this effect of inertia during sudden acceleration, making Option D the correct choice.

questions.text: What type of virus is the smallpox virus?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: These viruses have a DNA genome surrounded by a capsid and an envelope. They often establish persistent infections.Examples: Herpes simplex virus (Herpesviridae), Hepatitis B virus (Hepadnaviridae), Poxvirus (Poxviridae), Cytomegalovirus (Herpesviridae).

These viruses have an RNA genome surrounded by both a capsid and a lipid envelope. The envelope helps them enter host cells but makes them more sensitive to drying and detergents.Examples: Influenza virus (Orthomyxoviridae), Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV, Retroviridae), Hepatitis C virus (Flaviviridae), Rabies virus (Rhabdoviridae).

These viruses contain DNA within a naked capsid, without an envelope. They are usually very resistant to environmental stress.Examples: Adenovirus (Adenoviridae), Human papillomavirus (Papillomaviridae), Parvovirus (Parvoviridae), and Polyomavirus (Polyomaviridae).

These viruses have an RNA genome enclosed only within a protein capsid (no envelope). This makes them more stable in harsh environments.Examples: Poliovirus (Picornaviridae), Rotavirus (Reoviridae), Norovirus (Caliciviridae), and Hepatitis A virus (Picornaviridae).

questions.explanation: Viruses are classified by genome type and presence of an envelope. RNA-enveloped viruses (e.g., influenza, HIV) have RNA with a capsid and lipid coat, while RNA-non-enveloped viruses (e.g., polio, rotavirus) carry RNA only in a capsid. DNA-enveloped viruses (e.g., herpes, hepatitis B, and poxvirus) contain a large double-stranded DNA genome with both capsid and envelope, whereas DNA-non-enveloped viruses (e.g., adenovirus and papillomavirus) package DNA only in a protein shell. Notably, the poxvirus is a DNA-enveloped virus.

questions.text: The value of K.E is changed by change in temperature because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Reactants become more reactive than products
options.explanation: Temperature can influence reactivity, but this isn’t the direct reason why Kc changes.

options.text: The rate of reaction is increased
options.explanation: Temperature does increase the rate of reaction, but Kc is an equilibrium property, not a rate property.

options.text: The activation energy of reaction is decreased.
options.explanation: Activation energy is influenced by catalysts, not directly by temperature change.

options.text: Equilibrium position shift without any substance being added or removed.
options.explanation: This is correct. Temperature changes shift equilibrium according to Le Chatelier’s Principle, which in turn changes the equilibrium constant Kc​ because the ratio of products to reactants at equilibrium changes.

questions.explanation: The equilibrium constant Kc​ depends on temperature only (for a given reaction).When temperature changes, the system responds by shifting the equilibrium position to oppose that change (Le Chatelier’s principle).This shift alters the relative amounts of reactants and products even though no extra reactant or product was added — the driving force is purely the temperature change.Since Kc​ is the ratio of concentrations of products to reactants at equilibrium, this shift changes its value

Once the reaction has occurred, it is difficult to reverse it. For example, the combustion of methane in the presence of oxygen is an irreversible reaction: CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O.
This describes a reaction that occurs naturally without continuous external input, but it can still be reversible or irreversible, so it doesn’t specifically mean “one direction only.”

questions.text: Exothermic reactions are favoured in the forward direction by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Exothermic reactions are favoured in the forward direction by decreasing the temperature. This is because exothermic reactions release heat, so decreasing the temperature will help to drive the reaction forward by removing heat from the system.

Endothermic reactions are favoured in the forward direction by increasing the temperature. This is because endothermic reactions absorb heat, so increasing the temperature will help to drive the reaction forward by providing heat to the system.

Exothermic reactions are not favoured in the forward direction by adding a catalyst. Catalysts do not affect the position of equilibrium, so they do not affect whether an exothermic reaction proceeds in the forward or reverse direction.A catalyst is a substance that speeds up the rate of a chemical reaction without itself being consumed in the reaction. Catalysts work by providing a different pathway for the reaction to take place, a pathway that has a lower activation energy. This means that the reaction can proceed more quickly, but it does not change the equilibrium of the reaction.
Le Chatelier's Principle states that a system in equilibrium will shift in the direction that counteracts any change that is made to the system.

questions.explanation: According to Le Chatelier's principle, if a change is applied to a system at equilibrium, the system will shift to counteract the change. For an exothermic reaction (which releases heat), decreasing the temperature will shift the equilibrium to the right to produce more heat.

questions.text: According to law of mass action, the rate at which a reaction proceeds is proportional to the_of reactants.

options.explanation: The law of mass action specifically refers to the active mass of reactants, not their total mass. The active mass takes into account the concentration or amount of reactants that are available to participate in the reaction.
The law of mass action states that the rate of a reaction is proportional to the active mass of the reactants.

Molar mass refers to the mass of one mole of a substance, typically expressed in grams per mole. It is not directly related to the rate of a reaction, as the law of mass action concerns the active mass or concentration of reactants.

Molarity is a measure of concentration, which relates the amount of solute (reactant) to the volume of the solution. While concentration is related to the active mass, the law of mass action focuses on the active mass itself rather than the specific concentration unit.

questions.explanation: The law of mass action states that the rate of a reaction is proportional to the active mass of the reactants.

questions.text: A disc revolves in a horizontal plane at a steady rate of 3 rad/s. A coin, when placed on the disc at 20 cm from the axis of rotation, just starts to slip. The coefficient of friction (g = π2 m/s2) is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. This value is too high. Re-evaluate the balance between centripetal and frictional forces.
Incorrect. This value does not align with the calculated values based on the given parameters.

This is the following solution: Ff= μ Fn Since the coin is just about to slip, the maximum value of static friction (Ff-max) is acting on the coin, and it is equal to: Ff-max = μ Fn-max Now, the normal force (Fn-max) can be calculated as the gravitational force acting on the coin: Fn-max = mg where: g is the acceleration due to gravity (given as π2 m/s2). Now, substituting the expressions for Fc and Fn_max into the equation for static friction: μ mg = m ω2 r Solving for the coefficient of friction (μ): μ = (m ω2 r) / (m g) m and m (mass of the coin) cancel out from both sides:μ = (ω2 r) / g Now, we can plug in the given values: ω = 3 rad/s r = 0.2 meters g = π2 m/s2 μ = (32 x 0.2) / π2 μ ≈ 0.18 Therefore, the coefficient of friction (μ) between the coin and the revolving disc is approximately 0.18.
Incorrect. This value is too high and indicative of an error in calculation.

Now, substituting the expressions for Fc and Fn_max into the equation for static friction: μ mg = m ω2 r Solving for the coefficient of friction (μ): μ = (m ω2 r) / (m g) m and m (mass of the coin) cancel out from both sides: μ = (ω2 r) / g Now, we can plug in the given values: ω = 3 rad/s r = 0.2 meters g = π2 m/s2 μ = (32 x 0.2) / π2 μ ≈ 0.18 Therefore, the coefficient of friction (μ) between the coin and the revolving disc is approximately 0.18.

questions.explanation: To solve the problem, we use the following steps:1. The horizontal velocity (Vx) remains constant at 9.8m/s, as there is no acceleration in the horizontal direction.2. The vertical velocity (Vy) can be calculated using the formula Vy = gt, where g = 9.8m/s2.3. We set the horizontal velocity equal to the vertical velocity: Vx = Vy = 9.8m/s.4. questions.text: A body is moving along a straight path. What will happen to the body in the absence of an external field?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Without an external force, there is no reason for the body to stop. Newton's first law states that an object will continue in its state of motion unless acted upon by an external force.

options.text: It will move with the same speed in a different path
options.explanation: Changing direction requires an external force. Since no external force is present, the body cannot change its path according to Newton's first law.

options.text: It will move with the same speed along the same straight path
options.explanation: This is correct. Newton's first law of motion states that an object in motion will continue moving at the same speed and in the same direction unless acted upon by an external force. In this scenario, no external force is acting on the body.

options.text: It will move with a reduced speed along the same path
options.explanation: The speed of the body will not reduce without an external force acting on it. According to Newton's first law, it will continue moving at a constant speed.

questions.explanation: According to Newton's first law of motion, an object in motion will remain in motion with the same speed and direction unless acted upon by an external force. Option C is correct because it states that the body will continue moving with the same speed along the same straight path. Option A is incorrect because stopping requires an external force, which is absent in this scenario. Option B is incorrect because changing direction also requires an external force. Option D is incorrect because a reduction in speed would require an external force to decelerate the body.
